privateRanges.use: Single Class A block — large enterprise networks
16 contiguous Class B blocks — mid-size networks
256 Class C blocks — home and small office (RFC 1918)

specialRanges.name: This network
specialRanges.purpose: Refers to the local network; 0.0.0.0 also means 'any address' when binding sockets or as a default route.

specialRanges.name: Loopback
specialRanges.purpose: Local host — 127.0.0.1 always points back at this machine. Packets never leave the kernel.

specialRanges.name: Link-local (APIPA)
specialRanges.purpose: Auto-assigned when DHCP fails. Seeing this on a host usually means DHCP is broken.

specialRanges.name: Multicast
specialRanges.purpose: Class D — one-to-many delivery (OSPF 224.0.0.5, mDNS 224.0.0.251, IPTV streams).

specialRanges.name: Reserved
specialRanges.purpose: Class E — reserved for future / experimental use; not routable on the public internet.

specialRanges.name: Broadcast
specialRanges.purpose: Limited broadcast — sent to every host on the local segment, never forwarded by routers.
